blocked.explanation: Use conduct research, not make research.
Conduct research is the standard academic collocation.

blocked.example: do a study -> conduct a study
blocked.explanation: Conduct a study is the natural academic collocation.

blocked.example: make a study -> conduct a study
blocked.explanation: Use conduct a study for research design.
Statistical data is the natural adjective-noun form.
Big is informal; check whether significance is statistical or general.
Substantial effect is more academic, but check evidence strength.
Particularly important is a natural academic intensifier.
Interpret findings is more natural; examine findings may also fit.

blocked.example: give a result -> produce a result
blocked.explanation: Produce a result or yield a result is more natural.
Collect data is the standard research collocation.
Conduct an analysis or carry out an analysis is natural.
Conduct an analysis is more academic than do analysis.

blocked.example: find a result -> obtain a result
blocked.explanation: Obtain or report a result is more natural, depending on context.

blocked.example: say a finding -> report a finding
blocked.explanation: Report a finding is the natural academic expression.

blocked.warning: Review context before applying; this fix may affect meaning.
blocked.explanation: This may need plural agreement and evidence-sensitive wording.

blocked.example: students learn better -> students reported improved understanding
blocked.warning: Review context before applying; this fix may affect meaning.
blocked.explanation: This revision avoids inventing learning gains; review against evidence.
